2606.05107.title: Who Needs Labels? Adapting Vision Foundation Models With the Metadata You Already Have
2606.05107.paper_summary: We propose a label-free approach to adapt powerful but generic vision foundation models to specialized scientific domains. Standard supervised fine-tuning is often ill-suited to these settings: labels are scarce, and task-specific training can collapse the model's generality and hurt robustness. We instead leverage metadata to adapt representations to new domains in a self-supervised manner. Our method, FINO, combines a standard self-supervised objective with flexible metadata guidance that handles both highly granular discrete metadata and continuous metadata. It encourages the representation to preserve informative factors while suppressing spurious ones. Across subcellular fluorescence microscopy, Earth observation, wildlife monitoring, and medical imaging, FINO consistently outperforms standard unsupervised domain adaptation and fully supervised adaptation. It also exceeds highly-specialized domain-specific state of the art, while using no task labels for backbone adaptation and only lightweight probes for supervision.
2606.05107.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一種無標籤的方法，以將強大但通用的視覺基礎模型適應於專門的科學領域。標準的監督微調通常不適合這些環境：標籤稀缺，且特定任務的訓練可能會削弱模型的通用性並損害穩健性。我們則利用元數據以自我監督的方式將表示適應到新領域。我們的方法FINO結合了標準的自我監督目標和靈活的元數據指導，能夠處理高度細化的離散元數據和連續元數據。它鼓勵表示保留信息因素，同時抑制虛假因素。在亞細胞熒光顯微鏡、地球觀測、野生動物監測和醫學影像等領域，FINO始終優於標準的無監督領域適應和完全監督的適應。它還超越了高度專業化的領域特定最先進技術，同時在骨幹適應中不使用任務標籤，並僅使用輕量級探針進行監督。
2606.05107.author: Elouan Gardès et.al.
2606.05107.authors: Elouan Gardès, Seung Eun Yi, Kartik Ahuja, Théo Moutakanni, Huy V. Vo, Piotr Bojanowski, Wolfgang M. Pernice, Loïc Landrieu, Camille Couprie
